tldr: Day 8 (Week 2) of MAE onboarding. **R006–R009 are now 7 days stale** — unblocking even one lets the night-builder resume. Side project today: **morning-brief**.
tldr_short: MAE Week 2 Day 8. R006–R009 7 days stale — unblock one to restart night-builder. Side project: morning-brief.

items.title: MAE: deep-dive eval framework code
items.desc: Read lib/* evaluator source — map the 29 evaluators (17 rule + 10 LLM judge + 2 hybrid). Prep 3 questions for Jun Li.

items.title: Unblock R006–R009 (7 days!)
items.desc: Reply to at least ONE of R006–R009 so the night-builder can resume. Quick wins: R008 (Crumbs PR) or R006 (Vercel deploy).

items.title: Skim BenchGuard paper
items.desc: Automated auditing of LLM agent benchmarks — directly relevant to MAE eval quality. 15 min read.

paper.title: BenchGuard: Who Guards the Benchmarks? Automated Auditing of LLM Agent Benchmarks
paper.authors: Xinming Tu, Tianze Wang, Yingzhou et al.
paper.tldr: BenchGuard uses frontier LLMs to systematically audit agent benchmarks — finding that many 'agent failures' are actually broken specs, implicit assumptions, and rigid eval scripts.
paper.why: Directly maps to MAE's 29 evaluators: are the eval scripts themselves sound? This paper gives a framework to audit eval infrastructure before trusting the scores.
key_findings: Many apparent agent failures are actually benchmark bugs — broken specs or rigid evaluation scripts
Frontier LLMs can serve as systematic auditors of evaluation infrastructure
First automated auditing framework for task-oriented, execution-based agent benchmarks

items.title: OpenAI models coming to Amazon Bedrock
items.summary: OpenAI and AWS CEOs announce OpenAI models available on Bedrock — the multi-model API strategy accelerates.
items.why: ByteDance uses multiple model providers; Bedrock access changes the competitive landscape for enterprise AI infra.

items.title: Who owns the code Claude Code wrote?
items.summary: Legal analysis of IP ownership when AI coding agents generate code — murky territory for employers and developers.
items.why: Directly relevant: you use AI agents daily for coding. Understanding IP implications protects your side projects.

items.title: AI asked to count carbs 27,000 times — never the same answer twice
items.summary: Rigorous consistency test: LLMs gave wildly different answers to identical carb-counting queries across 27K trials.
items.why: Classic LLM-judge consistency problem. MAE's 10 LLM-judge evaluators face the same reliability challenge at scale.

items.question: R006–R009 have been open for 7 days. Can you reply to at least one today?
items.context: The night-builder is completely idle because all side-project repos are frozen. Even one answer unblocks meaningful autonomous work. Quick options: R008 (Crumbs PR — yes/skip) or R006 (Vercel deploy — pick a/b/c/skip).
options: I'll answer on the feedback page today
Skip all — freeze side projects for now
